title: Fix angle-bracket generics in Ferrum code blocks
description: Several docs use Rust-style angle brackets (Vec<i32>, HashMap<K,V>, Result<T,E>) in Ferrum code blocks instead of the correct square brackets (Vec[i32], HashMap[K,V], Result[T,E]). Ferrum uses square brackets exclusively for generics. Affected files found so far: ferrum-introduction-to-iterators.md (heavily affected — dozens of instances), ferrum-introduction-for-rust-programmers.md (Rust comparison blocks are fine, but check Ferrum blocks), compiler-agent-api.md, ferrum-PLAN-FAST.md, SemanticQuery-protocol-spec.md. The rule: angle brackets in type position in a ferrum code block are wrong and should be square brackets. Angle brackets in rust code blocks for comparison are correct and should not be changed.
